a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan:
Energy intensity is measured as
primary energy
consumption per unit of gross domestic product (GDP), in

kilowatt-hours
per dollar. GDP is adjusted for inflation and differences in living costs between countries.
Data source: U.S. Energy Information Administration (2023); Energy Institute - Statistical Review of World Energy (2024); Bolt and van

Note: GDP is measured in
international-$
at 2011 prices.
